message:
feat(conformance): add backtrace_ops executors (bd-wec8)

Add execute_* handlers for backtrace_ops family:
- backtrace: calls ABI, returns positive_count if frames captured
- backtrace_symbols: stub (needs valid frame addresses)
- backtrace_symbols_fd: stub (would write to fd)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-3u0","title":"Hard-parts workload matrix: map real binaries to required subsystems","description":"Critique mapping: #1 + #4.

Deliverables:
- Curate representative binary matrix (coreutils/python/git/curl/etc) with required subsystem dependencies.
- Use matrix to prioritize rtld/nss/locale/resolver milestones by real-world unblock impact.

Acceptance:
- Every hard-part milestone references at least one blocked workload from matrix.
- Matrix used by bv/priority report for automatic next-task ranking.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","notes":"2026-02-11 closure (CrimsonCove): workload_matrix_test (6 pass) + gate script. Workloads have required fields, subsystem impact consistent, milestones reference valid workloads.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCove","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:11.032905409Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T16:54:16.707976Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T16:54:16.707976Z","close_reason":"Hard-parts workload matrix operational. 6 harness tests pass. check_workload_matrix.sh validates workload definitions, subsystem impact, and milestones.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","e2e","roadmap"]}
 {"id":"bd-3v3","title":"Harness: asupersync deterministic orchestration for conformance + runtime_math scenarios","description":"Integrate /dp/asupersync into glibc-rs-harness runner.

Scope:
- Deterministic scheduling, traceability ids, structured results.
- Orchestrate scenario runs that exercise runtime_math controllers.

Acceptance criteria:
- Harness runner uses asupersync primitives for reproducible runs and evidence logging.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CobaltForge","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:35:42.149982753Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T05:21:29.149784585Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T05:21:29.149765789Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":1022,"issue_id":"bd-3v3","author":"CobaltForge","text":"Implemented asupersync-backed deterministic conformance orchestration in glibc-rs-harness.

- Added `glibc_rs_harness::asupersync_orchestrator` (feature `asupersync-tooling`) to run fixture verification deterministically (stable fixture-set + case ordering), assign stable `trace_id`s, and emit structured evidence via `asupersync_conformance::{SuiteResult, TestResult, Checkpoint, ConformanceTestLogger}`.
- Fixed harness runner bug where fixture cases with `mode=both` were executed with mode=both (unsupported) instead of the active runner mode.
- `harness verify` now sorts fixture JSON paths deterministically and uses the orchestrator when `asupersync-tooling` is enabled; when `--report` is provided it additionally writes `<report>.suite.json` via `asupersync_conformance::write_json_report`.

Quality gates: `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo check --all-targets`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo test --all-targets` all pass.
","created_at":"2026-02-10T05:21:20Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3vtx","title":"bd-1rf subtask: hosts files lookup subset implementation + explicit scope boundaries","description":"Background:
- hosts lookups are a practical dependency for many workloads and must be deterministic in files-backend scope.

Goal:
- Implement hosts files lookup subset with explicit scope boundaries and error semantics.

Deliverables:
1) Deterministic hosts parsing and lookup.
2) API mapping for supported resolver-facing calls in files scope.
3) Explicit non-goals for networked NSS backends.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Covered host lookup fixtures pass.
- Out-of-scope behavior is explicit and documented.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit tests for IPv4/IPv6 and malformed hosts entries.
- Structured logs: trace_id, query, family, result_source, outcome, timing.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CloudyRobin","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:01:53.995225917Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-15T19:03:24.895427810Z","closed_at":"2026-02-15T19:02:54.796760654Z","close_reason":"Implemented and verified (hosts files subset)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","implementation","nss","resolver","testing"],"comments":[{"id":1023,"issue_id":"bd-3vtx","author":"CloudyRobin","text":"Implementation + verification evidence for `bd-3vtx`:

Implemented scope (files backend only; explicit non-goal for DNS/networked NSS fallback):
- `crates/frankenlibc-core/src/resolv/mod.rs`
  - added `getaddrinfo_with_hosts(...)`
  - retained numeric resolution path
  - added deterministic hosts-content lookup path and family filtering
  - explicit no-network fallback behavior (`EAI_NONAME` when unresolved in files subset)
- `crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/resolv_abi.rs`
  - wired `/etc/hosts` subset resolution in ABI `getaddrinfo` for non-numeric names
- `crates/frankenlibc_conformance/src/lib.rs`
  - conformance executor supports optional `hosts_content` input for `getaddrinfo`
- `tests/conformance/fixtures/resolver.json`
  - added `getaddrinfo_hosts_file_subset` fixture

Commits carrying this work:
- `2a6d8aa` feat(resolv): add /etc/hosts file-backend lookup for getaddrinfo
- `ebb96f5` test(conformance): add hosts-file getaddrinfo resolver fixture

Verification (offloaded using `rch`, remote worker `vmi1152480`):
1. `RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=true rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-core getaddrinfo_with_hosts -- --nocapture`
   - Result: PASS (4 tests)
   - `getaddrinfo_with_hosts_ipv4_lookup`
   - `getaddrinfo_with_hosts_respects_family_filter`
   - `getaddrinfo_with_hosts_case_insensitive_alias`
   - `getaddrinfo_with_hosts_no_network_fallback`
2. `RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=true rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc_conformance execute_getaddrinfo_case_uses_hosts_subset_when_provided -- --nocapture`
   - Result: PASS (1 test)

Operational note: an initial `rch` run in the active shared workspace hit rsync churn due concurrent file activity by other agents and attempted local fallback; I stopped those runs and re-ran in an isolated temporary worktree to keep execution fully remote/offloaded.
","created_at":"2026-02-15T19:02:16Z"}]}

- Bench delta and update regression thresholds if justified.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GentleOwl","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:32:26.472452403Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T18:52:52.602069532Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T18:52:52.602051328Z","close_reason":"32 tests passing: unit tests for safe/unsafe points, property tests (monotone risk/depth/adverse sweeps, no-panic extremes), kernel integration, deterministic regression golden, hot-path integer-only evidence. 708 total tests, clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-45d","depends_on_id":"bd-19h","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-45d","depends_on_id":"bd-242","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-45d","depends_on_id":"bd-d5l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-ule","title":"EPIC: New runtime_math kernels (FDR, SOS, localization, Groebner, approachability, Sobol, proof-carrying policy)","description":"Goal: add new alien-artifact kernels that compile down to tiny deterministic guards/dispatch, without blowing hot-path budgets.

Kernels in scope:
- alpha-investing / sequential FDR controller (controls false escalations under many monitors).
- SOS-derived barrier certificates (offline synthesis; runtime polynomial eval).
- Atiyah-Bott localization fixed-point policy chooser (offline fixed points; runtime selection).
- Groebner-basis normal-form canonicalization of violation signatures (offline basis; runtime reduction table).
- Blackwell approachability controller for multi-objective (latency, risk, coverage) set convergence.
- Sobol low-discrepancy probe scheduler (deterministic coverage acceleration).
- Proof-carrying policy tables (offline synth; runtime table lookup + hash/cert checks).

Non-negotiable:
- Each kernel must specify hot-path cost, update cadence, cached state surface, and a test strategy.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:30:15.520777833Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T02:11:54.998226285Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T02:11:38.823974086Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-1v2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-2vf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-2wd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-3ld","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-45d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-abi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-cv9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-gn9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-l2r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1145,"issue_id":"bd-ule","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Epic Notes: New runtime_math Kernels (How They Serve libc)

### Purpose
runtime_math is the control plane for the membrane: it decides when to stay fast vs go deep, and when to repair vs deny. New kernels are allowed only if they compile to **small deterministic runtime artifacts** (tables, tiny evaluators) with *explicit* overhead.

### Reverse Core Map Anchoring (Surface -> Failure Class -> Math -> Runtime Artifact)
These kernels must tie to concrete libc pressure points:
- **Hot string/memory kernels** -> overlap/alignment/dispatch faults -> localization + proof-carrying tables -> certified dispatch tables
- **Allocator** -> temporal/provenance corruption + contention regimes -> approachability + SOS barrier -> admissibility guard + multi-objective controller
- **Loader/symbol/IFUNC** -> compatibility drift -> Groebner normal forms + localization -> canonical “violation signatures” + resolver table witnesses
- **Conformance monitoring (many tests, many monitors)** -> multiple-testing false escalations -> alpha-investing FDR -> controller that caps false alarms across families
- **Harness probe selection** -> coverage gaps -> Sobol -> deterministic low-discrepancy probe schedules under budget

### Branch-Diversity Reminder (Project Rule)
Major milestones must include at least:
- conformal statistics: already in-tree (`conformal`, `eprocess`) + this epic adds FDR
- algebraic topology: already in-tree (`cohomology`, `persistence`)
- abstract algebra: this epic adds Groebner
- Grothendieck-Serre methods: already in-tree (`higher_topos`) + localization is in the same “geometry/localization” vein

### Runtime Cost Discipline (Non-Negotiable)
For each kernel we require:
- hot-path invocation budget (ns/op) and cadence (per call vs per epoch)
- cached state surface (what lives in `RuntimeKernelSnapshot`)
- deterministic arithmetic (fixed-point if possible; no heavy transcendentals)
- tests that show behavior invariants and that perf gates remain within budget

### Deliverable Shape
Each kernel follows the same pipeline:
1. Design bead: math contract + loss model + artifact format + invariants
2. Implement bead: tiny runtime evaluator/controller (no heavy machinery)
3. Integrate bead: wire into `RuntimeMathKernel::decide/observe` with versioned snapshot fields
4. Tests/perf bead: microbench + property tests + regression thresholds","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:51:50Z"},{"id":1146,"issue_id":"bd-ule","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CobaltCompass: All 9 blockers now CLOSED. Epic complete: alpha-investing FDR, SOS barriers, localization chooser, Groebner normal form, approachability, Sobol, proof-carrying policy tables all integrated with tests and perf validation. 809 membrane tests pass.","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:11:54Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-vj3","title":"Perf: Per-kernel microbench suite (criterion groups + thresholds)","description":"Add a criterion microbench suite that measures *per-kernel* costs, not just end-to-end decide().

## Why
End-to-end `decide()` can hide regressions. Per-kernel benches let us pin blame when a new kernel or refactor shifts cost.

## Bench Design
- Criterion group per kernel (or per family of kernels).
- Measure:
  - `decide` contributions (hot modules)
  - cadence update costs (epoch modules)
- Report ns/op and p95/p99 where applicable.

## Dependency
- Must reuse the baseline harness from `bd-d5l` and regression thresholds from `bd-242`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A new kernel cannot land without adding/adjusting its microbench.
- Thresholds catch >X% regressions (configurable) in CI.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BrightMoose","created_at":"2026-02-09T22:03:30.833286241Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T01:27:48.417613055Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T01:27:39.911611946Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vj3","depends_on_id":"bd-242","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-vj3","depends_on_id":"bd-d5l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-vj3","depends_on_id":"bd-pt6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1147,"issue_id":"bd-vj3","author":"BrightMoose","text":"Added per-kernel microbench binary crates/glibc-rs-bench/benches/runtime_math_kernels_bench.rs (machine-readable RUNTIME_MATH_KERNEL_BENCH lines) and wired it in crates/glibc-rs-bench/Cargo.toml.

Also hardened scripts/perf_gate.sh for dev environments:
- skip under extreme system load (GLIBC_RUST_PERF_SKIP_OVERLOADED=1, GLIBC_RUST_PERF_MAX_LOAD_FACTOR=0.85)
- optional kernel-suite run flag (GLIBC_RUST_PERF_ENABLE_KERNEL_SUITE=1) collecting RUNTIME_MATH_KERNEL_BENCH lines.

Baseline comparisons for kernel-suite metrics not yet enforced (needs baseline schema + stable host load to record p50s).","created_at":"2026-02-10T17:32:28Z"},{"id":1148,"issue_id":"bd-vj3","author":"CobaltCompass","text":"Verified complete by CobaltCompass. BrightMoose delivered per-kernel bench with 6 criterion groups: risk_upper_bound_ppm, bandit_select_profile, barrier_admissible, pareto_recommend_profile, design_choose_plan, approachability_observe/summary. Machine-readable RUNTIME_MATH_KERNEL_BENCH lines for CI gating. Wired into Cargo.toml and perf_gate.sh (GLIBC_RUST_PERF_ENABLE_KERNEL_SUITE=1). Note: per-kernel baseline enforcement deferred to stable host load recording.","created_at":"2026-02-11T01:27:48Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vq1g","title":"D.1: (SUPERSEDED) — folded into parent bd-9dq5","description":"## Goal
Implement the request-tracking infrastructure for async DNS and the gai_error/gai_cancel functions.

## Design

### Request Registry
A global mutex-protected HashMap<usize, AsyncDnsRequest> maps gaicb pointer addresses to request state. The gaicb struct (defined by POSIX) contains:
- ar_name: hostname to resolve
- ar_service: service name/port
- ar_request: hints (addrinfo struct)
- ar_result: output addrinfo list (written on completion)

### gai_error(req: *mut gaicb) -> c_int
1. Look up req address in registry
2. If not found: return EAI_SYSTEM (unknown request)
3. Return state: EAI_INPROGRESS (still running), 0 (completed successfully), EAI_CANCELLED, or the error code from getaddrinfo

### gai_cancel(req: *mut gaicb) -> c_int
1. Look up req in registry
2. If not found: return EAI_NOCANCEL
3. If already completed: return EAI_ALLDONE
4. If in progress: set CANCELLED flag, return EAI_CANCELLED (or EAI_NOTCANCELED if thread already started resolution)

### State Constants
- EAI_INPROGRESS = -100 (glibc value)
- EAI_CANCELLED = -101
- EAI_ALLDONE = -103
- EAI_NOTCANCELED = -102

## Implementation
File: crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/unistd_abi.rs (near existing gai_* functions)
- Add AsyncDnsRequest struct
- Add static ASYNC_DNS_REGISTRY: LazyLock<Mutex<HashMap<usize, AsyncDnsRequest>>>
- Replace gai_error body
- Replace gai_cancel body

## Testing
- gai_error on unknown request returns EAI_SYSTEM
- gai_cancel on unknown request returns EAI_NOCANCEL
- Register a fake completed request, gai_error returns 0
- Register a fake in-progress request, gai_cancel returns EAI_CANCELLED

## Acceptance Criteria
State machine is correct. Registry tracks requests. Tests pass.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:19:44.987634072Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T01:59:38.338086567Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T01:59:38.337881268Z","close_reason":"Superseded: Track D redesigned as synchronous-only.  {"id":"bd-w2c3.9.2","title":"T8.2 Deterministic E2E Packs: smoke/stress/fault/stability/security","description":"## Background

Changes:
- structured_log now accepts legacy artifact-index join-key aliases (`trace_id`, `span_id`, `decision_id`, `policy_id`, `evidence_seqno`) and normalizes them into canonical plural arrays.
- artifact indexes missing `run_id`/`generated_utc` now deserialize with deterministic legacy defaults via `normalize_legacy_defaults()`.
- evidence_compliance emits a WARN proof-log event (`evidence_compliance.artifact_index_legacy_defaults`) when legacy defaults are synthesized, instead of failing older v1 bundles.
- added schema/migration coverage in `structured_log_test` and `evidence_compliance_test`, including the canonical `log_schema.json` artifact-index example and a legacy index without `run_id`.

Verification:
- `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test structured_log_test --test evidence_compliance_test`

  Expected: workspace check passes.
  Observed: PASS.
- `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc cargo fmt --check`
  Observed: FAIL due pre-existing formatting drift in `crates/frankenlibc-abi/build.rs` (not this slice).
- `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`
  Observed: FAIL due pre-existing `clippy::manual_is_multiple_of` in `crates/frankenlibc-membrane/tests/alien_cs_e2e_test.rs:170` and `:173` (outside this slice).

Failure triage:
1. Legacy artifact-index bundles missing `run_id` should now produce a WARN proof-log event, not an `artifact_index.invalid_json` parse failure. Inspect `evidence_compliance.proof.log.jsonl`.
2. Singular legacy join-key fields should serialize back out as canonical plural arrays. Inspect `structured_log_test` roundtrip output if this regresses.
3. Remaining workspace fmt/clippy failures are unrelated repo drift; inspect the files above before blaming this bead.","created_at":"2026-04-06T23:01:40Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-wuh","title":"EPIC: RaptorQ-inspired runtime resilience (erasure-coded evidence + metadata self-healing)","description":"Goal: use RaptorQ patterns accretively inside libc runtime, without adding /dp/asupersync as a runtime dependency.

Core idea (pattern, not copy-paste):
- Systematic fast path: store source symbols (raw evidence) cheaply.
- Repair path: generate deterministic repair symbols on a cadence (epoch boundary), not per call.
- Decode + proof: decoding and explainability happen in diagnostics/harness, not on the strict hot path.

Candidate uses:
1) Erasure-coded evidence ring buffer for runtime_math decisions/heals/anomalies.
2) Self-healing for small critical metadata capsules (headers/roots), not entire large tables.
3) Cross-shard summary reconstruction to support cohomology/consistency monitors under partial loss.

Success criteria:
- Strict fast path unchanged; hardened adds bounded overhead only when enabled.
- Deterministic encoding parameters + evidence-ledger tuning of redundancy.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:30:15.573551832Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T03:03:13.254498204Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T03:03:13.145127019Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-wuh","depends_on_id":"bd-284","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-wuh","depends_on_id":"bd-2xr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-wuh","depends_on_id":"bd-3a9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-wuh","depends_on_id":"bd-3ku","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-wuh","depends_on_id":"bd-pc4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1215,"issue_id":"bd-wuh","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Epic Notes: RaptorQ-Inspired Resilience Inside libc Runtime

### North Star
Turn fragile “best effort” telemetry into **recoverable, explainable evidence** under partial loss, while keeping libc hot paths fast.

RaptorQ is the reference pattern because it gives us:
- systematic fast path (zero overhead when nothing is lost)
- deterministic repair generation (no coordination required)
- recovery from erasures (dropped/missing records) with small overhead
- explainability via decode proofs (in tooling)

### Non-Negotiable Constraints
- No `/dp/asupersync` in the runtime dependency graph.
- Strict mode hot path stays “boring”: constant-time, no allocations, no locks.
- Hardened mode overhead is bounded and measured (microbench + regression gates).

### Concrete libc Use Cases (Scope)
1. **Erasure-coded evidence ring**
   - Evidence includes: runtime_math decision + key context + membrane outcome + heal action + stage exits.
   - Goal: if we drop N% of events or overwrite parts of the ring, tooling can still reconstruct a consistent prefix / windows.
2. **Self-healing critical metadata capsules**
   - Small, high-value state snapshots (kernel snapshot schema, policy tables hash, config epoch) should survive partial corruption.
3. **Cross-shard reconstruction for consistency monitors**
   - Cohomology/consistency monitors can treat “missing overlap witnesses” as erasures and reconstruct summaries.

### Runtime Design Principles (Extreme Optimization)
- Evidence recording per call:
  - `seq = atomic_fetch_add(Relaxed)`
  - store a fixed-size record into a per-thread ring slot
  - no heap alloc
  - no syscalls
  - no GF(256) operations
- Repair symbol generation:
  - done on cadence: every `K_source` events, per-thread or global epoch
  - uses deterministic seed; work amortized
  - may run in a background worker thread only in hardened mode
- Export:
  - tooling/harness reads the ring (or a frozen snapshot) and performs decode + proof.

### Reliability Model
We model losses as:
- erasures: records not present (ring overwrite, sampling drop, partial capture)
- corruption: payload hash mismatch (bit flips, races)

We treat corruption as “erase and try to repair” (don’t let bad bytes poison decode).

### Adaptive Redundancy (Control)
- Observe: loss rate (erasures) and corruption rate in exported traces.
- Decide: raise redundancy when anytime-valid evidence says we’re violating durability/observability budgets.
- Act: increase `R` (repair symbols) in future epochs; optionally retro-harden recent epochs.

This is implemented via `bd-284` and wired to runtime_math e-process + evidence ledger.

### Deliverables / Done Means
- Encoding path exists with strict-mode overhead indistinguishable from baseline.
- Offline decode exists and verifies record integrity + hash-chain.
- Loss/corruption simulation tests demonstrate reconstruction up to documented budgets.
- Evidence reports are explainable: when repair happens, tooling emits a DecodeProof.","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:50:51Z"},{"id":1216,"issue_id":"bd-wuh","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"EPIC COMPLETE. All 5 blockers resolved: bd-3a9 (architecture), bd-pc4 (offline decoder), bd-2xr (loss simulation), bd-3ku (evidence integration), bd-284 (adaptive tuner). Evidence ring buffer integrated, adaptive redundancy tuner with e-process + audit ledger, 69 modules, all quality gates passing.","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:03:13Z"}]}
